title: hieracium villosum subsp. calvifolium
intro: Hieracium villosum subsp. calvifolium, also known as woolly hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hieracium villosum subsp. calvifolium include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
